dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7914 IH: Agricultural Conservation Mentorship Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. McClain Delaney (for herself and Mr. Vindman) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Food Security Act of 1985 with respect to the experienced services program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Agricultural Conservation Mentorship Act.
Section 1252 of the Food Security Act of 1985 (16 U.S.C. 3851(a)(2)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (D), by striking ; and and inserting a semicolon;
in subparagraph (E), by striking (as defined in section 1404 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3103)). and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

assisting cooperative initiatives under section 1417(b)(3) of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3152(b)(3)) to improve higher education teaching programs, including paid work-based learning, at institutions of higher education.

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:
The term institution of higher education has the meaning given that term in section 101 of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (20 U.S.C. 1001).
The term land-grant colleges and universities has the meaning given that term in section 1404 of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3103).
The term work-based learning has the meaning given that term in section 3 of the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 (20 U.S.C. 2302).
